channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Alex Bregman finally finds new home in Red Sox after strenuous free agency: reports
item.description: Alex Bregman is reportedly in agreement to join the Boston Red Sox on a three-year deal worth $120 million after spending his first nine seasons with the Astros.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fueled by grudge, Jamal Murray scores career-high 55 points in Nuggets' win over Trail Blazers
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The only thing hotter than Jamal Murray's jumper on Wednesday night might have been his temper. The Denver guard was still carrying a grudge against the Portland Trail Blazers over being ejected for some trash talking on Monday.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet Colorado's top scorer: Olathe junior Isaiah Gordon
item.description: No player in the entire state of Colorado scores more points per game than Olathe junior Isaiah Gordon.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Canada defenseman Shea Theodore is out for the rest of the 4 Nations Face-Off because of injury
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — Canada defenseman Shea Theodore is out for the rest of the 4 Nations Face-Off because of injury, coach Jon Cooper said after his team beat Sweden 4-3 in overtime in the first game of the NHL-run tournament, calling it a big blow.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: George’s 22 points help lead Georgia Tech past Stanford 60-52
item.description: Stanford's Raynaud, who is averaging a team-high 20.4 points per game, was held to eight points on 4-of-13 shooting in Atlanta Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Alex Bregman and Red Sox agree to $120 million, 3-year contract, AP source says
item.description: Alex Bregman and the Boston Red Sox have agreed to a $120 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the deal tells The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Briscoe gives Toyota its first Daytona 500 pole as Johnson and Truex also lock into NASCAR opener
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Chase Briscoe led a banner night for Toyota in qualifying for the Daytona 500 by winning the pole for NASCAR's “Great American Race.” It is the first Daytona 500 pole for Briscoe, who moved to Joe Gibbs Racing this season when Stewart-Haas Racing closed at the end of 2024.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nick Pivetta agrees to $55 million, 4-year contract with San Diego Padres, AP source says
item.description: Nick Pivetta became the last of the high-profile free agent pitchers to reach an agreement this offseason, a backloaded $55 million, four-year contract with the San Diego Padres, a person familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Villanova edges No. 9 St. John's 73-71 to stop Red Storm's winning streak
item.description: VILLANOVA, Pa. (AP) — Tyler Perkins hit a go-ahead 3-pointer with 8 seconds remaining and Villanova defeated No. 9 St. John’s 73-71 on Wednesday night, ending the Red Storm's 10-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bengals react after Chilean men indicted, take selfie with stolen items in robbery of Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The NFL star's residence was burglarized last December when he was in Arlington, Texas, for a game against the Cowboys, investigators confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mets finalize Pete Alonso deal and re-sign injured reliever Drew Smith
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pete Alonso and the New York Mets finalized their $54 million, two-year contract on Wednesday as spring training opened in Florida with the team's first formal workout for pitchers and catchers.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Quick return to Torrey Pines brings familiar setting with a lot more of golf's best players
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The corporate boxes around the 18th green at Torrey Pines are just like they were three weeks ago except for the color — black instead of blue — and the logo. The rough is thicker from recent rain.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Girls Soccer: University City 4, Madison 0
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)-Girls soccer University City beat Madison 4-0 on senior night for the Warhawks. Sofia Christensen scored twice for the Centurions.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: BYU Athletic Director Tom Holmoe announces retirement after 20 years
item.description: According to the BYU, Holmoe will be retiring at the end of the 2024-2025 athletic season, after one of the "most successful football seasons" of BYU.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: NFL fans used this much mobile data at Raymond James Stadium this season
item.description: NFL fans used a staggering amount of mobile data at stadiums this past season, according to data from Verizon.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl returning to the Bay Area for 2026
item.description: (KRON) -- The Super Bowl is coming back to the Bay Area next year. A decade ago, the region hosted Super Bowl 50. Next year, it will play host to Super Bowl LX. On Monday morning, San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan was in New Orleans for the official Super Bowl handoff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC earns first shootout victory over Capitals
item.description: WASHINGTON D.C. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is carrying a ton of momentum into the Four Nations Break. Karel Vejmelka made 30 saves in relief, and the Utah Hockey Club recovered for a 5-4 shootout win against the Washington Capitals at Capital One Arena on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl losers' merch passed to other nations
item.description: The NFL plans for both outcomes so they're ready regardless of who wins. What that inevitably means is a whole lot of merchandise that's pre-printed and pre-made – but fans won't ever be able to buy it.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Clippers to sign former top pick Ben Simmons: report
item.description: A former No. 1 overall pick in the NBA Draft has found a new home in Los Angeles. The L.A. Clippers are expected to sign three-time All Star Ben Simmons as a free agent when he clears waivers, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Here's how to watch the Super Bowl for free
item.description: The Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles will face off in Super Bowl LIX, two years after their matchup in Super Bowl LVII, which the Chiefs won 38-35.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, NewsNation

item.title: Public visitation scheduled for Virginia Halas McCaskey
item.description: Virginia Halas McCaskey, the 102-year-old matriarch of the Chicago Bears, will be remembered on Tuesday, Feb. 11, at an event held by Oehler Funeral Home in Des Plaines.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Blackhawks take losing streak into matchup with the Predators
item.description: Nashville Predators (18-27-7, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (16-31-6, in the Central Division) Chicago; Friday, 8:30 p.m. EST BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks play the Nashville Predators after losing three straight games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Raiders legend Fred Biletnikoff keeps daughter's dream alive
item.description: LOOMIS, Calif. - Fred Biletnikoff still goes to a lot of Raiders games. "Sometimes I don't know why," laughed the Hall of Fame wideout. "It's just the history." Fred has a lot of history with the Raiders.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9

item.title: Antonio Gates, Jared Allen, Eric Allen and Sterling Sharpe make the Pro Football Hall of Fame
item.description: Antonio Gates, Jared Allen, Eric Allen and Sterling Sharpe were voted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame in the smallest induction class in 20 years following offseason rule changes meant to make it harder to get inducted.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5
